title: Passport Reset Ledger Entry
description: One append-only ledger entry in reset_boundary[]. Two kinds: 'boundary' (recorded at a FULL checkpoint when ARS_PASSPORT_RESET=1) and 'resume' (recorded when resume_from_passport consumes a boundary entry). See academic-pipeline/references/passport_as_reset_boundary.md.

kind.description: Discriminator. 'boundary' = reset emitted at a FULL checkpoint.

hash.description: First 12 lowercase hex characters of SHA-256 over the concatenated ledger (prior entries + this entry with hash set to the canonical placeholder '000000000000'). Placeholder rule is normative — see protocol doc §'The reset boundary protocol' step 2.

stage.description: Stage number or name that just completed (e.g., '1', '2', '2.5', 'RESEARCH').

next.description: Default next stage after resume. Advisory when pending_decision is set — the orchestrator must re-prompt the user before using next.

session_marker.description: Opaque identifier for the source session. Audit-only; not relied on for resume.

version_label.description: Monotonic version label for the passport state at this boundary (e.g., 'v1.0', 'v1.1-revised'). Stage re-runs bump this.

verification_status.description: Mirror of Schema 9 verification_status at this boundary. Copied for convenience; authoritative value lives in Schema 9 top-level.

observer_snapshot_ref.description: Optional path or ID of the collaboration_depth_agent observer output for this checkpoint. Audit-only.

question.description: Human-readable question the user must answer on resume (e.g., 'Stage 3 review outcome: revise, restructure, or abort?').

options.description: Enumerated decision branches. Must be unique — chosen_branch on the resume entry is a bare string, so duplicate labels would make the append-only audit trail ambiguous. Orchestrator must prompt the user to pick one before auto-advancing.

pending_decision.description: Set when the boundary co-occurs with a MANDATORY user decision (Stage 3 rejection, Stage 5 finalization, etc.). When present, resume must re-prompt the user; 'next' is advisory.

kind.description: Discriminator. 'resume' = resume_from_passport consumed a prior boundary entry.

consumes_hash.description: Hash of the boundary entry this resume consumes. Append-only pointer — lets readers compute awaiting_resume state from the ledger alone.

session_marker.description: Opaque identifier for the resuming session.

chosen_branch.description: If the consumed boundary had pending_decision, record which option the user chose. Omit otherwise.

user_override.description: Optional stage/mode overrides the user supplied on the resume command.
